steps.title: Make the x-coefficient vanish
steps.content: Factor $x$ from the left side: $$-3x+18px=(-3+18p)x$$ The equation becomes $(-3+18p)x=90$. Since $90\ne0$, there is no solution only when the coefficient of $x$ is $0$, producing the contradiction $0=90$.

steps.title: Solve p with a coefficient regression
steps.content: Set $-3+18p=0$. Desmos can solve that condition directly with a one-row regression and returns $p=\frac{1}{6}$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: With $p=\frac{1}{6}$, the equation becomes $0x=90$, which is impossible. All other choices leave a nonzero coefficient on $x$ and therefore give exactly one solution. That matches choice D, so D is correct.
